entry.title: Re: CORUPROC transaction not clearing AFFW data
entry.content: DELETE affw FROM TABLE affw_d. * erfolgreich ? IF sy-subrc EQ 4. DESCRIBE TABLE affw_d LINES tmp_entries. MESSAGE x803 WITH 'AFFW' tmp_entries sy-dbcnt. ENDIF.

entry.title: Quick Sizer tool
entry.content:
We have been using quick sizer tool to estimate the CPU memory , but the tool is not clear and not provided the correct information. 

Used the Non-HANA-based Classic Quick Sizer option to download and use the Quick Sizer tool.

Questions:

  1. In the sizing tool shows Crystal Report 2016 instead of 2020. Is it possible to change to 2020 version?
  2.  Once filled and runs the tools, it only shows "Results show "Medium CPU - 64GB memory" , but customer requires a detailed breakdown of:
    1. How many required virtual CPUs?
    2. How many RAM allocation?
    3. Is it possible to have more detailed hardware specifications is needed?
  3.  Is it possible to print and/or save as PDF?

 

Followed instructions on how to use the Quick Sizer tool to calculate CPU, disk, memory and I/O resource for your BI 4.x environment - SAP for Me

Please advice. Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

Used the Non-HANA-based Classic Quick Sizer option to download and use the Quick Sizer tool.

    2. How many RAM allocation?
    3. Is it possible to have more detailed hardware specifications is needed?
  3.  Is it possible to print and/or save as PDF?

 

Followed instructions on how to use the Quick Sizer tool to calculate CPU, disk, memory and I/O resource for your BI 4.x environment - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: Error in outbound IDoc: "EDI: Field ARCKEY in control...

entry.title: Re: Integration Center demo for the Compensation Module
entry.content:
For our organization, both the performance rating and the talent rank are updated in the employee's profile through the extended user import process, which occurs once the performance cycle and calibration cycle have been completed and finalized.

We do not maintain a compensation history section within the employee's profile that encompasses both the historical rating and ranking; instead, these are kept in separate sections or blocks within the profile. Additionally, employees are allowed to view their rating, while their ranking remains hidden from them.

During each compensation cycle, we manually upload the current and previous year's ranking values along with the rating value from the prior year. The rating value for the current year is sourced from the performance management form.

Given this process, is it possible to utilize the MDF feature in a non-EC enabled instance, or do you have any suggestions for minimizing the manual workload in the compensation worksheets? Please inform me.

entry.title: Re: Integration Center demo for the Compensation Module
entry.content:
@xavierlegarrec , 

For our organization, both the performance rating and the talent rank are updated in the employee's profile through the extended user import process, which occurs once the performance cycle and calibration cycle have been completed and finalized.

We do not maintain a compensation history section within the employee's profile that encompasses both the historical rating and ranking; instead, these are kept in separate sections or blocks within the profile. Additionally, employees are allowed to view their rating, while their ranking remains hidden from them.

During each compensation cycle, we manually upload the current and previous year's ranking values along with the rating value from the prior year. The rating value for the current year is sourced from the performance management form.

Given this process, is it possible to utilize the MDF feature in a non-EC enabled instance, or do you have any suggestions for minimizing the manual workload in the compensation worksheets? Please inform me.

entry.title: How to delete bgRFC units automatically?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

How to delete or Remove error units from bgRFC queue automatically without any Manual intervention.   

We know the process to delete this unit manually using SBGRFCMON however we would like to know any automated  process is available for this task.

Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to delete bgRFC units automatically?
entry.content:
Hello, 

It's possible to schedule the program RS_BGRFC_MASS_OPERATIONS in background mode for daily bgRFC units deletion. For more information about report, refer to SAP Note below:

3018243 - Mass delete or unlock bgRFC units in background

Regards

entry.title: Re: Currency conversion insufficient privilege
entry.content:
Hello @TheFizzlee 

You're getting the "insufficient privilege" error in PROD because the currency conversion tables (TCUR) weren’t shared* from the SAP space to the PROD space, even though they were shared to TEST.

How to fix.

  • Go back to the Currency Conversion Views Creator
  • Select the SAP space as the source
  • Click “Share” and select the PROD space

This ensures PROD has access to the TCUR tables behind the views; otherwise, imports will work, but runtime access will fail.

Let me know if it works

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: BTP Background Job Scheduling | Consume RAP Service from BTP (Node JS)
entry.content:
This is a question from the customer.  Can we use SAP BTP Job Scheduler to schedule jobs in SAP S/4HANA SM37.

Meaning we create the job in S/4HANA SM37.   Scheduling this should be done in SAP BTP Job Scheduler.. The Customer is trying to replace RunMyJobs by Redwood with SAP BTP Job Scheduler 

Is this possible, I keep hearing that BTP Job Scheduler is used for BTP Jobs alone can you please help me uderstand more.

entry.title: Re: Traslape en tabla de cuotas patronales para Cesantia y Vejez
entry.content:
Hola Hugo,

Revise este tema con el área de negocio y su postura es que el valor correcto es aplicar el 4.954% para el supuesto planteado. 

Anexo los comentarios:

En realidad, en la práctica los primeros 4 rangos no tienen aplicación, hasta el quinto con un 5.307 %

El IMSS no permite dar de alta a un empleado con el salario mínimo de $278.80 es decir sin su respectivo factor de integración mínimo de ley, por lo tanto, el SBC mínimo es de $292.54 y nos aplica el 5.307% en esa rama.

María Elena Ramírez

Saludos.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics cloud planning - Data action issue
entry.content:
Hi @pragnya_m ,  if you have the periods being maintained as the properties of version dimension, here is an example how to filter out the data in data action formulas. 

More details can be found in help doc:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/afe93e3cf1414a7b8419baad11cc066e.html#loio4ab31f8c107a427387da65eb7c9fccbb

entry.title: Re: Service Order WITH BATCH
entry.content:
Hello @AngelT 

You're getting the "Enter Lote" error because the material is batch-managed, and the system requires a batch number during service confirmation.

Next step

  • Use the “Manage Service Confirmations” app, and the batch field ("Lote") is visible there.
  • Enter the batch number manually or select it using F4.
  • If the field is still missing, use the “Post Goods Movement” app and enter the batch during the 261 movement.

Let me know if it works

With regards

Chuma

I need to create a bank of data to test macroprocess in S4hana private cloud.

Thanks.

entry.title: Request for Business Role to License Type Mapping Document
entry.content:
Hi,

Please explain how to check the app names along with their associated license types, such as Advanced, Core, or Self-Service. 

Regards,

Silambarasan


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Request for Business Role to License Type Mapping Doc...
entry.content:
Dear Silambarasan,

The Price Category is related to Business Role and Business Catalog, but not for specific app.

To check the price category of business role and business catalog, you can use app "IAM Information System".

After you select the Main Entity as "Business Role" or "Business Catalog", there will be columns "Business Role Price Category" and "Business Catalog Price Category".

For more information about the price category and FUE, you can check the threads below:

Did you say FUE? A definition. - SAP Community

Understanding the Price Category information - SAP Community

Thanks and Best Regards,

Bell

entry.title: Re: PO Form Template Extend Custom Field to Limit Item Nodes
entry.content: Did you get the answer for this?

entry.title: Get Certified in SAP Sales Cloud V2 with Our Live Session Series!
entry.content:
Are you ready to take your career in sales technology to the next level? Do you want to become a go-to expert in implementing one of the most innovative sales solutions on the market? Then mark your calendars!

SAP Learning is thrilled to announce a brand-new live session series designed to help you ace the SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 exam!

Click here to explore the full series and register

Why SAP Sales Cloud Version 2?

In today's fast-paced sales environment, businesses need more than just basic CRM. SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 is a game-changer, empowering sales teams with:

  • AI and Machine Learning: Predictive analytics, intelligent recommendations, and automated insights to help reps prioritize leads and close deals faster.
  • Seamless Integration: Connects effortlessly with the broader SAP ecosystem (ERP, S/4HANA, etc.) for a truly end-to-end business process.
  • Modern User Experience: Intuitive, mobile-first design built on SAP Fiori for enhanced usability and adoption.
  • Flexibility & Extensibility: Low-code/no-code options and an API-first approach for easy customization and integration.

This isn't just about managing customer data; it's about driving intelligent engagement and accelerating business growth. As more companies adopt this powerful solution, the demand for skilled implementation consultants is skyrocketing.

Why Get Certified?

Earning your "SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP Sales Cloud Version 2" certification demonstrates your foundational knowledge and in-depth skills in implementing and administering this cutting-edge solution. It validates that you:

  • Possess a comprehensive understanding of SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 fundamentals.
  • Can actively participate in project teams focusing on Sales Cloud V2 implementations in a mentored role.
  • Are equipped to configure key sales-specific capabilities, manage master data, and understand extensibility options.
  • Understand critical areas like Lead and Opportunity Management, Activities Management, Mobile App capabilities, and the integration of Machine Learning and Generative AI.

This certification is your badge of excellence, differentiating you in the job market and opening doors to exciting career opportunities!

What You'll Get from These Live Sessions:

This exclusive series of live sessions, led by an SAP expert, is quite simply your best shot at prepping for success. You'll walk away with:

  • Comprehensive Coverage: This is a 6-part live session series, each about an hour long, designed to take you through the entire content of the learning journeys.
  • Deep Dives: Expert-led sessions covering the key topics and modules you'll need to master for the certification exam. You'll get an "expert's eye" on what's truly important for both the exam and real-world application.
  • Practical Insights: Get a feel for actual implementation scenarios and pick up best practices you can use right away.
  • Interactive Learning: Chat directly with SAP experts and get your burning questions answered live.
  • True Confidence: Step into your certification exam feeling completely ready and sure of your abilities.

Seriously, don't let this incredible chance to boost your skills and validate your expertise in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 slip by!

Check out the link below for the full schedule, all the session details, and make sure to register today:

https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/get-certified-sap-certified-associate-implementation-consultant-sap-sales-cloud-version-2

Invest in yourself and become the SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 implementation wizard everyone needs. Your next big career move is waiting!

entry.title: Re: Using SAVE_TEXT in VF01 for item texts
entry.content:
Sorry to post here after it mentioned solved, because my requirement is also related to this topic.

I am trying to update the settlement month in the item text of an invoice from the billing plan. Initially I have implemented the logic in the userexit RV60AFZC, which is triggered during invoice creation (VF01). However, after the invoice is created, the item text is not updated when I check in VF02 or VF03. It appears that the TDNAME in the SAVE_TEXT function does not contain the billing document number during invoice creation, causing the item text update to fail. then I tried on the RV60AFZZ under number range user exit same issue. 

Can you please guide me on this problem? 

Thanks.

entry.title: 2LIS_02_ITM did not get the deleted item of PO in ME22N ?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We deleted the items of PO using  t-code ME22N.

After that, clear and re-fill the setup table of 2LIS_02_ITM.

The key of transformation and 0RECORDMODE.

Run the DTP of FULL extraction.

Active table of ADSO still have the deleted item (EBELP = 03810) after activating the request.

Any step wrong or missing ?  Any mistake of transformation ?

Tks a lot for helping me.

entry.title: Re: [EWM] How to map hu level warehoust task to matdoc? (...
entry.content:
Hi,

If my understanding is correct, if you are seeking a connection between a task from EWM and a material document, you should be able to locate it in MKPF. The field SPE_MDNUM_EWM is concatenated with the warehouse number and task number. We will need to separate this information in the extraction code of the program.

Thanks

Mayur Sharma (mayurs819)

entry.title: 国内跨公司销售POD不生效
entry.content:
同一个公司下的销售组织和工厂，POD使用正常； 国内的跨公司销售（工厂和销售组织属于不同公司），POD不能使用；销售订单带出的移动类型为601，带不出687 。如下图：

 

IMG中“设置现货销售与 VSiT 流程的相关性”不允许配置工厂和销售组织属于不同公司的配置；如下图

entry.title: Re: 国内跨公司销售POD不生效
entry.content: 问题：国内跨公司销售（cross company sell）要使用POD，该怎么设置？

entry.title: Issue with Updating Item Text in Invoice Using Userexit RV60AFZC and RV60AFZZ in SAP
entry.content:
I am trying to update the settlement month in the item text of an invoice from the billing plan. Initially I have implemented the logic in the userexit RV60AFZC, which is triggered during invoice creation (VF01). However, after the invoice is created, the item text is not updated when I check in VF02 or VF03. It appears that the TDNAME in the SAVE_TEXT function does not contain the billing document number during invoice creation, causing the item text update to fail. then I tried on the RV60AFZZ under number range user exit same issue. 

Can you please guide me on this problem? 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Understanding ABAP Pipeline Engine used for Data Integration in Datasphere
entry.content: Excellent, Good to hear basic concepts of SLT and APE

entry.title: Re: How to restrict Q4 and B6 stock type at WPT level
entry.content:
Hi,

 

If you’re looking to restrict certain WPTs to be used for picking, you can drive it from the picking strategy. For instance, regardless of what picking reference document requests, the picking strategy only looks at the F2 stock type. This means that other stock types are skipped, and the task can only be created by a specific WPT with the F2 stock type.

 

If you’re looking to influence inbound receipt stock types to have only the F2 stock type, irrespective of the reference document, you can partially address this by having an availability group in the WPT to force the F2 stock type. However, this may not ensure that your entire requirement is met. Additionally, the storage type stock type should be maintained as mandatory with the value of FF stock type. This automatically flips the stock type even if the reference document says otherwise.

 

If your requirement is about internal tasks or ad hoc, I don’t think there’s a way to handle it in the standard. You may have to influence it through a BADI with system messages to restrict other stock types.

 

Thanks,

Mayur Sharma (mayurs819)

entry.title: Can I implement @ObjectModel.query.implementedBy in view entity, not custom entity?

entry.title: Re: Bank Statement MT940
entry.content:
Hi @Chuma,

Please confirm, can I create more than 1 account symbol?

Because the client has a lot of Bank accounts. and a different account code for bank incoming and bank outgoing.

Sample

Bank A:  1XXXX1 Bank Balance, 1XXXX2 Bank Incoming, 1XXXX3 Bank Outgoing

Bank B:   1XXXX4 Bank Balance, 1XXXX5 Bank Incoming, 1XXXX6 Bank Outgoing

Thanks,

Chanaporn S.

entry.title: Z migration object names generated for Z projects in Migration Modeler
entry.content:
Hi,

In app Migrate Your Data and tcode LTMOM, we can find the object technical names begins with 'Z'. Can you confirm if this is a normal acceptable behavior?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Z migration object names generated for Z projects in...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Yes, this is the standard behavior of data migration

You can check naming convention in KBA 3588725 - FAQ for migration cockpit. Migration approach: Transfer / Migrate data using staging tables (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition) - SAP for Me

  • Below is an example for a generated staging table for object Work center (SIF_WORK_CNTR) and structure S_KAZY

  • Table name: /1LT/DS<sys-id><consecutive number>

    • For example: /1LT/DSQKX000928

  • Table description: Z_<Alias = technical Object name without SIF_>_<Mass transfer ID>:<structure name>

    • For example: Z_WORK_CNTR_U02:S_KAZY

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Intercompany reconciliation - Transaction and company...
entry.content:
Hi @Gabor5,

This is correct.

Both sides (seller and buyer) recorded the transaction in consistent currency (WSL transaction currency) and amount, resulting in zero difference. When switching to their company code currency, the partner company has RON as their company code currency, which converted from 220 EUR. Hence the 2 lines listed by currency, and the difference is no longer comparable due to difference currency codes. See more info in Reconciliation Balances ( https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/90c07e91c7a64f328be3fd6b48955b13/68f41968f351435eb416acfecf8c4aa5.html).

Thanks!

Grace

entry.title: How to exclude hedged open items from foreign currency valuation using exchange rate fields?
entry.content:
Hello Gurus,

My question is:
Is there a way to configure foreign currency valuation to exclude open items with a manually fixed exchange rate, such as those covered by FX contracts?

Background:
In Japan, FX contracts are typically managed through allocation rather than SAP’s hedge management functionality. For open items (e.g., sales orders or invoices) that are already covered by FX contracts, we would like to exclude them from foreign currency valuation during month-end closing.

We noticed the field "Accounting Exchange Rate" is available in the document and are wondering if it can be used as a condition to exclude such items from valuation.

Attached screenshots for reference:

Sales Order screen – showing the manually maintained exchange rate

Journal Entry (Accounting Document) – showing the accounting exchange rate carried over

Foreign Currency Valuation result – showing that the item is still being evaluated

Any advice, experience, or workaround (even using enhancements or BAdIs) would be very helpful.

Thank you in advance.

FYI: We are using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (2408).

 Thank you

 Andre

entry.title: SAP Cloud Application Studio Deployment Failed in Production
entry.content: I attempted to activate my solution in the Implementation Manager; however, I consistently receive a "Deployment Failed" message. My internet connection is stable, and the deployment to the test tenant was successful. Unfortunately, I am unable to deploy it to the production tenant.

entry.title: SAP BTP runtimes, my personal considerations and preferences on Cloud Foundry, Kyma, ABAP runtimes
entry.content:
In this fast-paced environment... Nah, just kidding! This is not another AI generated or soulless marketing blog post plaguing the internet. This is about SAP BTP runtimes, and my personal preferences. I know, I know... There's an SAP badge next to my name, but it's because I couldn't keep my personal community profile detached from my SAP email due to reasons in the new community platform... So, ignore the badge, it's my own view, oversimplified and full of silly examples, not a recommendation nor official position from SAP. Don't read this and go back to your boss saying "hey, SAP is telling us that runtime A is better than B".

You may know that SAP BTP offers three runtimes where you can deploy and run your custom applications. We can pick and choose whatever we want. We can run everything in just one runtime, or, we can have workloads in all of them at the same time. You can adopt the runtimes according to your needs and/or skills.

There is always somebody asking me about the SAP BTP runtimes. What they offer, their limitations, their features, when to use this, how to use that. Sometimes they just want to confirm whatever they're using is right. Sometimes they have no idea they had options. I'd say this is a bit shocking for those coming from the ABAP on-premise world: “we have options!?!?!”.

 

* Runtimes / Environments

Yes, runtimes and environments are the same thing... No, I won't talk about Neo Environment because it's deprecated, it'll sunset on 31/12/2028.

Let's see a short description of runtimes before getting into more details.

Cloud Foundry Environment

"The Cloud Foundry environment enables you to develop new business applications and business services, supporting multiple runtimes, programming languages, libraries, and services. You can leverage a multitude of buildpacks, including community innovations and self-developed buildpacks."... from SAP BTP documentation

Kyma Environment

"SAP BTP, Kyma runtime provides a fully managed cloud-native Kubernetes application runtime based on the open-source project "Kyma". Based on modular building blocks, Kyma runtime includes all the necessary capabilities to simplify the development and to run enterprise-grade cloud-native applications."... from SAP BTP documentation

ABAP Environment

"The ABAP environment is based on the latest ABAP platform cloud release that is also used for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. It leverages the innovations provided by SAP HANA. The software stack contains standard technology components that are familiar from the standalone Application Server ABAP. The ABAP environment supports the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model including SAP Fiori and Core Data Services (CDS)."... from SAP BTP documentation

 

* Supported languages

The programming language you use can limit what you do and how fast you can deliver

Cloud Foundry Environment

CF supports pretty much any language as long as you have a buildpack for that. There are official buildpacks maintained by the Cloud Foundry Foundation, by SAP itself and others by the CF community. The Node.js buildpack is a good example.

Kyma Environment

Kyma also supports any language as long as you have a container to run it. Some languages have their official container images which you can use as-is or customize as you wish. The Node.js Docker image is a good example.

ABAP Environment

ABAP is quite obvious. It only supports ABAP, but not your old school ABAP report declaring internal tables like DATA: lt_mara TYPE TABLE OF mara WITH HEADER LINE. It only runs ABAP Cloud, a subset of the ABAP language tailored to modern days.

 

* Performance

In terms of performance, all runtimes can deliver great results. They'll be mostly limited by how your applications were built. Translation: if the application is too slow, your code is the problem, not the runtime.

Nevertheless, there'll be a threshold where even the highest quality app won't cope with high demand. There's no magic number, it varies. This is a hurdle overcome by good software engineering and scalability.

 

* Scalability / Elasticity

Scalability in CF and Kyma is quite flexible and granular. Vertical and horizontal scaling are available. You can automatically scale up and down your applications. No need to touch it, no manual monitoring, no manual resource allocation, no need to guess what the workload demand will look like next Wednesday at 4:20 AM AEST time.

Your cool AI powered app is being bombarded by incoming requests from all the customers accessing it? No problem, new instances can be created and destroyed based on rules such as memory or CPU usage. For example, if the app is consuming more than 90% of CPU, spawns a new instance to share the workload. If the CPU usage goes down, let's say under 50%, stop and kill the previously spawned instance.

You have 1000 apps, but just 1 app is under stress? No problem, you can scale up just that one app! CF and Kyma run everything in containers. They're fully isolated and have their own resources. For instance, app AAA has 1 GB memory available for itself, app BBB has 5 GB and app CCC has 256 MB. You can also change the allocated memory at any time. For instance: “oh, I thought my app would need 2 GB RAM, but it actually needs 1 GB”. No problems! Adjust it without changing the code nor redeploying the app.

You may have a rule dictating apps will always have at least 2 instances running to distribute the workload. This is actually a best practice and encouraged to be adopted. Perhaps you don't need an app with 6 GB memory, but 3 instances of the same app with 2GB each. Running multiple instances is how you enable high availability for your apps as well, if one instance crashes the others keep working. It also enables zero downtime deployments.

Oh, the beauty of the cloud! 😍 Can you imagine doing these things in your ALV reports running on SAP ECC 6.0?

Cloud Foundry Environment

The service Application Autoscaler provides everything you need to manage scaling your apps. It's just a configuration. If you have an app with 1 instance or 99 instances running, nothing changes for you as a developer, system admin or end user. CF Load Balancers take care of distributing the incoming requests between all the instances. It's transparent and super smooth. Just make sure to gracefully shutdown your apps to avoid serving requests from a stopping instance. I'd written and spoken about graceful shutdown before, have a look: blog post + video.

Kyma Environment

Pretty much the same as CF. You define the configuration rules, the load balancer takes care of distributing the traffic. You don't need to code this behaviour, the Keda module available in the runtime does that for you. Kyma is powerful, and much more flexible than other runtimes. You can scale to zero nodes. You can use heaps of scalers out-of-the-box. You can also create pretty much any custom rule: if it's Wednesday, and it's raining, and Bitcoin is under 100k, spin up a new instance ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

ABAP Environment

Ok, now we'll see a big difference. You cannot have multiple copies of your app as in CF/Kyma. You have no granularity as the ABAP environment resources are shared between all applications. Everything runs in "one" box, vertical scaling is the only option. You either bump the whole environment or don't, pretty much the same as in on-prem servers. Have a look at Elastic Scaling of the ABAP Application Server. Yes, the service supports High Availability.

 

* Resource isolation

Let's do a mental exercise to understand resource allocation and isolation a little bit more. Think about the following scenario. You have 100 apps running at the same runtime. You deploy a change to 1 of those apps. The code change has a bug! The app goes into an infinite loop, starts consuming 100% of CPU and becomes unresponsive. What happens to the other apps?

Cloud Foundry Environment

Nothing. The other 99 apps are running in their own containers, fully isolated with their own resources (limited memory and CPU per app). Only users using the buggy app will be impacted. Users accessing other apps won't have any issues.

Kyma Environment

Same as CF. Hooray to containers! 🍻

ABAP Environment

I'm sorry to say, but you messed up badly. All 100 apps will be unresponsive. All users, even the ones not using the app in infinite loop will be impacted. The bug just caused a company wide issue.

Let's stretch this example a bit more to make a point. Your company has 5000 users consuming those apps in SAP BTP ABAP runtime. The buggy app is a tiny noncritical one being used by 2 users from IT to import data from Excel to HANA (😉). Because the bug is impacting the whole ABAP runtime, everyone is affected, 5000 users will complain they can't use their apps.

Resources are shared here, there's no app isolation. You cannot say one app will use only 512 MB, one app can consume all resources available in the environment. As I said, it's the same behaviour as in SAP ABAP Netweaver servers. You can think about it as a big gaming desktop running ABAP.


* Developer experience (DX)

Let's be honest, DX was neglected for a long time in SAP. It started changing when ADT was introduced for Eclipse users. Nowadays, DX in SAP BTP can be great! People can use the same modern tools the cool kids are using out there.

Cloud Foundry Environment

When developing applications to run in CF, you focus on the code, not much on the infrastructure to run it. You write your code, build and deploy it. CF will get the request, build the container, put your app inside the container, prepare everything, execute the app and make it available for you.

You have a bunch of tools to use from SAP, from Cloud Foundry and from both communities. Endless options for pretty much everything!

A simple "Hello World" app can go like this: write the code (easy), prepare the manifest.yml (easy) or a mta.yaml (can be overwhelming) and push to CF (easy). The app is ready to be used.

Kyma Environment

Your attention is needed on both code and infrastructure. Have you ever worked with Kubernetes? It's the same. Deploying to Kyma can be a bit scary as its manifest.yml is even more complex than the mta.yaml file in CF. Kyma also requires you to manage the container you want to run your application in. You must build the container before deploying your app. You won't get anywhere without a good amount of knowledge.

Again. You have a bunch of tools to use from SAP, from Kubernetes and from both communities. Endless options for pretty much everything!

A simple "Hello World" app can go like this: write the code (easy), prepare the container image you want to use (it may involve multiple steps and can be hard, especially for beginners), prepare the manifest.yml (hard) and push to Kyma (easy).
The app is ready to be used.

ABAP Environment

You have no choice of IDE, you must use Eclipse with ADT plugin. No SAP BAS nor Build Code. All ABAP tools are provided by SAP. You need a fair amount of knowledge to get started. It might be intimidating for noobs without any ABAP skills.

You may find some open source projects, but let's be honest, other than ABAPgit, the ABAP open source community doesn't have much in terms of tooling. It's pretty much 3 absolute amazing guys caring the whole community on their shoulders: Lars Hvam, Marcello Urbani and Marc Bernard.

A simple "Hello World" app can go like this: write the code (kinda easy, too many moving pieces, too verbose), push to ABAP Environment (kinda easy), activate it (easy). The app is ready to be used.

 

* Skills / Skill set

It's quite obvious, but for all the three runtime options, you need to know the language you want to use, eg. Node.js or Java or Python, and the tooling for that specific language (linting, code quality, automated testing, profiling, etc).

Recruiters complain they can't find resources, that there are no SAP BTP Developers out there. IMO, they're looking in the wrong place, developers don't need to speak SAP to work on SAP BTP. A SAP BTP Developer is “just” a developer who knows any modern language and cloud platforms. Get a Java developer with Kubernetes knowledge and they'll be deploying to SAP BTP on the same day. Youngsters in universities know at least one of the languages behind CAP (Node.js/Java) and play with cloud platform/services everyday. They're way ahead in the cloud game than ABAPsaurs like us 🥲

Cloud Foundry Environment

Git is a must! You can get far with little CF knowledge. You don't have to know containers, eg. Dockers, but it can be handy. It's the most beginner friendly of all runtimes.

Kyma Environment

Git for sure! Containers are a must, you won't do anything without knowing Dockers or similar. The learning stage can be challenging, it requires knowledge in many new concepts at once. You must know Kubernetes. It can be a bit harder to debug issues due to more moving parts.

ABAP Environment

Look at you! ABAP also requires git! You must know ABAP Cloud and ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP). Old school ABAP knowledge may be helpful, but even for skilled ABAPers it can be challenging. For beginners, it can be a nightmare.

The RAP level required is not like singing along with Ice Cube in It Was A Good Day:

ABAP RAP is more like Eminem in Rap God when he reaches supersonic speed (verbose is an understatement):

 

* DevOps

Both CF and Kyma have plenty of options. You can use SAP BTP services like SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery / SAP Cloud Transport Management. A Jenkins plugin from SAP called Project Piper. Or, anything else that you already use in the company. Are you using MS Azure DevOps? No problem! You can keep using your CI/CD pipelines there to test and deploy your apps to SAP BTP. Are you a big fan of GitHub Actions? Great! Just use that.

ABAP doesn't give you many options. You can use the ones provided by SAP.

Regardless of the DevOps platform you're using, I'd strongly recommend SAP Cloud Transport Management (CTMS) for deployment. Don't do manual deployments or use CLI tools for that. The CI/CD process may run in non-SAP DevOps platforms, but deployment to SAP BTP should go through CTMS. You can integrate external CI/CD pipelines, eg. Azure DevOps or CircleCI, to CTMS via APIs, or, with my open source project for GitHub Actions: Deploy to SAP BTP with Cloud Transport Management Service (CTMS). With CTMS, you have full control on the changes going through the landscape, and have a proper audit log to trace them if required.

 

* Limitations

As everything else in the universe, runtimes have limits. They may not be a problem, but you should know and consider them when adopting any runtime. Again, you don’t have to stick to one runtime only. Use whatever combination works best for you.

This isn’t a comprehensive list, it’s only the main ones for me…

Cloud Foundry Environment

Doesn't offer serverless (Kyma does), it doesn't scale to zero (Kyma does), the closest option to scale to zero is turning the application off which means users won't be able to use it. Limited control on the number of CPUs in an application. For instance, you cannot have 10 CPUs allocated to your Hello World app.

Kyma Environment

Cold starts can be slow…

ABAP Environment

ABAP is the only supported programming language. OData is the only protocol. SAP HANA is the only database. Same as CF, it doesn't scale to zero. It doesn't support any offline development work, so, I have to find something else to do during the eternal hours when flying from Australia to other countries.

 

* My picks

What and why. Direct and unapologetic (sorry 😬).

Cloud Foundry Environment

This is my default choice! It's rare to see a requirement that can't be delivered with CF. I can be very productive and do more in less time because I'm mostly focusing on the code, nothing else. This gets even better with CAP as it adds another layer of abstraction and less code/work is required to get things done. More time solving business issues, less writing boilerplate code. Yes, you can also use CAP with Kyma.

Things to consider. If the team is new to SAP BTP, and cloud development in general, this is the easiest way to get started. If there're Java or Node.js developers in the company, they can easily start working with CF. No need to speak SAP!

Kyma Environment

I pick Kyma when hyperscalability is required, or, when I need to fiddle with the network layer, or, when CPU intensive workloads are expected and multi-thread is required. Productivity can also be high, but requires a team a bit more mature.

Another variable to consider is: does my team already know Kubernetes and uses it in other platforms such as AWS or MS Azure? If the answer is yes, then I'd pick Kyma over CF as the knowledge is transferable and they can start working straight away.

ABAP Environment

CF and Kyma give me so many options in terms of languages, tools and ways to build applications that I find it hard to have fun programming ABAP…

If my team only knows ABAP, nothing else, then ABAP might be the best runtime. But again, ABAP Cloud isn't the same as the old school ABAP. Sometimes it's easier to learn CAP and CF than RAP. The main point to consider here: don't let current skills restrict future technology choices.

 

* Final thoughts

As I said, this is my personal opinion, oversimplified to fit in a longer than expected blog post. I still love ABAP, but I believe we have better options now. Perhaps my love for Cloud Foundry is nostalgia from the old times using Heroku...

If you want a better comparison between Cloud Foundry and Kyma in SAP BTP, have a look at this documentation from SAP: Comparison: SAP BTP, Kyma Runtime and SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Runtime

entry.title: Re: [SAC] - How to set the Story filter's active members?
entry.content: I would assume this is NOT possible at the moment with story filter. I would suggest if you have can use page filter( input control) instead, you can manually sync the selection between page filters cross pages with API.

entry.title: Re: Send data from SAP Analytics Cloud to 3rd Party tools using CPI

entry.title: SAP- Desktop application login error
entry.content:
Hi,

We are encountering a login error during certain user login attempts, as attached screenshot. This issue appears sporadically and is not consistent across all sessions.

I have confirmed that the user is not logged in elsewhere, and as the connection is established via LAN, network issues have been ruled out.

Despite the error message, all modules continue to function as expected. The only impact observed is that the main dashboard screen displays the error.

Please let us know the solution.

entry.title: Re: How to Identify Documents Posted via MIR7, FV60, FV50, FV65, F-65 Without Attachments
entry.content: SRGBTBREL is the right table to check the documents with GOS.  If you want to check through which tcode, it has been executed, either you can go to aut10 or use table CDHDR in se16n

If you have additional questions in creating a support case, you can find more details in the SAP Signavio Onboarding Resource Center.

KantriceD

entry.title: Re: How to cancel individual tasks in Onboarding and Offb...
entry.content:
@BrandyPelzel 

1.There are some optional tasks in our onboarding and offboarding programs that were not completed and it doesn't make sense to have a manager complete them now that they are a couple of months old. They still show up on the manager homepage. 

Purge does not help in this case(Archival rule)? kindly refer below guide for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-onboarding/implementing-onboarding/archive-onboarding-offboarding-tasks

2.How can we cancel or remove those individual, old tasks without canceling the entire program for those new hires and departing employees? We would like to make a regular process to clean up outdated and incomplete tasks. What would you recommend?

Please refer above link

Thanks,

Gopi

entry.title: Re: Understanding ABAP Pipeline Engine used for Data Integration in Datasphere
entry.content: @bheemeshRao very well summarized and gives a good understanding of how SLT work and its dependencies. Thanks

entry.title: Re: Email in SF
entry.content: @Łukasz6 not possible

entry.title: Re: Workflow approval rebate and commission
entry.content: Go through this blog

entry.title: Re: Position Workflow Comment is Auto Filled
entry.content:
Hi @RishabhGupta19881 

In SF, workflow comments may be auto-filled during initiation due to configured workflow rules or business rules. These rules can automatically populate comment fields based on predefined logic, such as the user initiating the workflow or other relevant data.

Check the workflow rules associated with the specific workflow and the relevant workflow steps. Look for rules that might be automatically adding comments based on the initiator or other criteria.

If pre-defined comments are not require to auto-populate during initiation then you shall able modify/remove the block from the workflow rules.

 

Thanks

Arun

entry.title: Allowance Creation and Activation Upon Subscription Contract Submission in SOM
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We would require the allowance to be automatically created and activated in Convergent Charging (CC) at the time the Subscription Contract is submitted in Subscription Order Management (SOM).

Despite following the SAP Help guidelines, the allowance is still not being generated. The steps taken so far are:

  • Created an Allowance Plan with the Service Identifier set to "ALLOW".
  • Defined the Allowance Component as a 'one-shot' rate.
  • Created a Mapping Table Class with "ALLOW_DEF_ID" as input, and "ALLOW_DIF_DESC", "BucketQuantity", and "Validity" as outputs.
  • Maintained the Mapping Table and assigned it as a parameter in the Charge Plan.
  • Created an Allowance Definition Group and assigned an Allowance Definition ID with a released status.
  • Enabled CCM with the Allowance Definition Group ID, using "Full Table" as the Assignment Rule.
  • Created a Subscription Order, expecting the allowances to be reflected in the item, but no allowances are appearing in the SOM order.

Could you please help identify if any specific steps have been missed? I’m looking for guidance on how to ensure the allowance is created correctly via SOM upon the submission of the initial order.

Thanks in Advance.

entry.title: Re: System copy using SWPM Refresh or Move Database Insta...
entry.content:
Hi Shawn,

The error looked like HANA Database server refused the connection.
"System call 'connect' failed, rc=10061:No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it "

Have you  checked the network communication between the SWPM server and HANA Database server?
e.g. telnet hostname port

I could find this KBA maybe helpful, even though, this is not secondary connection but default connection, you may still refer to the resolution section for troubleshooting.
3543496 - Secondary connection to HANA DB failed with 10709 + 10061 error - SAP for Me

br, Rainie

entry.title: Re: employee time custom fields
entry.content:
Hi @amcwalter 

Custom Fields can be configured in "Employee Time" object with required data type. And it also can be enabled for everyone or any user specific roles. 

To populate the same field values in ESS UI/Employee profile, create a field in the required portlet with same data type created in Employee Time Object. Enable the related RBP if required.

Create a integration job in Integration Centre and set the Source object as "Employee Time" and Destination object as required ESS portlet then do a field mapping for User ID and the custom field in "Destination Object" from "Source Object"

Set the time-based  filter as based on "Modified Since".  The IC job can be ran manually or scheduled based on your requirement. 

Post successful run, you shall able to view the information flow to ESS portal from Employee time if user updated any information in the custom field. 

Thanks 

Arun

entry.title: Re: I want to create product code is 01010004BK when crea...
entry.content: My case alphanumeric sequence is not in order, so I haven’t tested it yet. Based on your suggestion, are the number ranges from A000000000 to A999999999 covered?

entry.title: Re: How to Identify Documents Posted via MIR7, FV60, FV50, FV65, F-65 Without Attachments
entry.content:
Thanks Lakshmpathi,

The requirement is to identify all postings made using the given transaction codes along with their corresponding attachments.

To achieve this, I extracted data from FBL1N and concatenated the company code, billing document number, and fiscal year. I then attempted to locate the related entries in the SRGBTBREL table. However, the expected records are missing, and I’m now unsure where to find the attachment linkage.

Appreciate your guidance on this.

entry.title: Re: Story - How can I get the latest incumbent of a posit...
entry.content:
Hi @lukaszkubiak 

The report appears to retrieve Current Incumbents for the Position, along with Employees who were terminated while holding that Position.

However, it will not include Employees who are still Active but currently assigned to a different Position.

To ensure the report only reflects Active Current Incumbents, applying a filter on Employee Status = Active within the Incumbents object should help achieve the desired outcome.

Thanks,

Raghu

entry.title: Critical: SAP Desktop Agent Randomly Disconnecting in VM – Impacting Automation Execution
entry.content:
The SAP Desktop Agent in the VM is getting disconnected automatically at random intervals, with no consistent pattern. As a result, the automation stops unexpectedly, and users are left waiting for results that won’t be generated unless someone manually logs into the VM. This is impacting the reliability of the process and needs attention urgently.

I've attached two images to illustrate the issue:

  • The first image shows the agent in a disconnected state when no one is logged into the VM.

 

  • The second image shows that once I manually log in, the agent status changes to "Disconnected->Preparing", and only then does the automation start working.

entry.title: Re: How to restrict Q4 and B6 stock type at WPT level
entry.content:
Hello Mayur,

Thanks you for your response, Yes we are looking for internal (Ad-hoc) movement ex. /SCWM/ADHU. The source bin is normal bins and destination bin is fixed every time ex. Staging bin.

Thanks,

Vinod

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI MFA with Microsoft Entra (Part II): Integration with Microsoft Entra Private Access
entry.content:
Thank you！

I want to know whether the user's local PC needs to be added to a domain when SAP GUI SSO is implemented in this way.

entry.title: Credit Note Adjustment through API in Public cloud
entry.content:
Sabari has various credit note adjustment scenarios being received against customer invoices. This data is being received from their internal DMS application via CP and being posted into SAP through standard APIs. There are many scenarios on how the data can be received:

  • One credit note adjusting against one customer invoice
  • One credit note adjusting against multiple invoices of the same customer
  • One invoice adjusting against multiple credit notes of the same customer

When the data is received, it is split into separate sets, and each set is individually processed and posted to SAP. We found that the same credit note or invoice may be duplicated in different scenarios. Hence, the Customer Account has to be cleared in SAP after each scenario is processed, to ensure the exact balances are reflected.

Sabari is receiving data from multiple DMS systems (different Supplier Dealer Management Systems), and to process the Credit Note Adjustment data for 1 supplier and 1 day, they have to clear the customer accounts at least 4 times for each Business units ( 6 Business units x 4 times = 24 times manual clearing on  a daily basis) . This is too much manual process at the moment, and we are looking at the best options to automate this. 

APIs

Thanks,

Vignesh

entry.title: Re: (Datasphere/SAC) Time Dimension attributes not visibl...
entry.content:
Hi again,

Yeah, deleting the chart didn't help much either. However, been digging around and it seems there's currently some issues with the actual native time table in DSP and the way SAC interprets the date data.

See this SAP Note (3324920) 

Even though I tried to wire my model like the note mentioned, using the "Data, string" field instead of the Data_SQL, it still didn't work.

I was able to solve my specific issue though, by making a hierarki with only the month dimension, applying it to my chart. That enabled me to view my current year/last year data in the same chart, on the month timeline.

entry.title: Re: How to get error message from odata while using smart table and smart filter .
entry.content:
Hello there,

at the  moment I have the same problem: I raise an exception in my backend OData service and I can see the error thrown with the browsers development tools when I work with the app...but how can I display this message in a MessageBox?

I already tried with an ErrorHandler.js example, but it didn't work...where do I need to call the "attachRequestFailed" Method? Which event of the smart table do I need to use for that?

Thank you,

Christopher

entry.title: Re: SAP Enable Now
entry.content: Thanks Shane for sharing reference blog post.

entry.title: Automation for cheque bounce process in SAP public cloud
entry.content:
Sabari is in the wholesale and distribution industry and has collection data across various branches across the states of Kerala and Tamil Nadu. The collection data is posted daily via API calls. Some of the payments are received via Cheques and they are posted similarly to the other payment receipts. The Customer account will be cleared after the payment is received.

After the checks are presented to the Bank, in a few cases, the checks may bounce. This data has to be reversed in the customer Accounts receivable that has already been posted and cleared in SAP. We have to reset and reverse the cleared items for a case with a bounced check. We have an API available to reverse the posted documents, but there is no option to reset the cleared items with this API. Currently, Sabari is managing this manually and they have a volume of 75-100 cheques per day across all the branches, which may be bounced.

APIs API Management SAP Intelligent Robotic Process Automation 

Thanks,

Vignesh

entry.title: Re: How to Identify Documents Posted via MIR7, FV60, FV50, FV65, F-65 Without Attachments
entry.content: It is not sure as to why some documents are missing in SRGBTBREL table.  Check SAP note 2746890

entry.title: Re: SAP C4C SDK 2505: New Visual Studio 2022 based Setup
entry.content:
Hi Saurabh,

I think you are trying to create a new project from the VS2022 startup page. Don't do that. Please click on the 'Continue without code' link/button at the bottom to open the Visual Studio IDE. Then, under 'View' menu click on 'My Solutions' to open the tool window. In the 'My Solutions' window, click on 'Log On' button to login to your system.

 

Best Regards,
Sasi.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori tools May 2024 release adds closer integration with ABAP Development Tools
entry.content:
Just wanted to confirm if this is only available for:

  • SAP BTP ABAP Environment
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

But NOT available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition/RISE?

entry.title: Rise With SAP
entry.content:
Hi,

Currently you are all aware about new SAP Business Transformation Buzz word going on by changing SAP on Premise Version to Rise With SAP (Public Cloud / Private Cloud Edition). It is gradually going towards making it mandatory for SAP Customers to move on to this new version on following ground:

ECC to S4 Hana Contract Conversion: Customer who had earlier converted contract from ECC to S4 Hana which is normal that we pay some fees for conversion. Normally we do not read the contract line items as we are under the impression that we have got right to use new version of SAP. This has turned into disaster as now it is not consider as license conversion. It is termed as you have ECC License for Hana use and not S4 Hana. This is totally absurd and whom to blame is a real question. This is true for many of the customers.

New On-Premise Licenses: All of us are aware that we do negotiation when we finalize the license deal. In current times there is no interest in selling on premise licenses. Even if we want to procure the prices are 10 time what we would have procured earlier. This really hurts the IT Budget & Company budgets in terms of ERP spend.

Now come the major part that as we do not have option available, we need migrate our contract from On Premise to Rise with SAP.  In this we will are surrendering our perpetual license right which we had procured and will have to pay new fees on subscription model.

The new license definition in Rise with SAP is on the basis of FUE which are determined on the basis of authorization object / activity. The same is classified as HB Professional (Ratio 1 User = 1 FUE)  / Functional (1 Fue = 5 User) / Productivity (1 FUE = 30 User). This is getting automatically determined via SAP Note 3113382.

The point which I want to highlight is that the definition which is decided on the basis of authorization object / activity is getting changed very frequently which is disturbing entire calculation of FUE count. In our case we had done the FUE Calculation basis this note with version 89 which was having list of authorization object / activity specified for classifying the user under 3 different lic. category. This definition has gone for change on 5th of June'2025 where certain object / activity which were earlier classified under category of Functional user has now got changed to Professional. So, from ratio of 5 it is has come down to 1. 

The definition change is in hand of SAP. If you just, try to evaluate the customer who had finalized the FUE and have already moved to Rise. Suddenly they will find increased number of FUE count as per the new definition so without increasing in number of users they have to pay additional cost to SAP.

This is very disturbing fact as it is clearly violation of trust which we had been carrying with SAP for so many years.  There is also known risk that as we will be moving to cloud (Public or Private) the renewals rates will be scary. We will not have any chance of negotiation which means what ever the rates which SAP wants to charge can be charge without any question ask as your entire data / landscape is in their hands unless you take a difficult decision in terms of moving to other software platform.

Aforesaid pointers need to be seriously looked into. 

Will appreciate if you can provide some insight on this.

Regards

Ketan

entry.title: Re: Quickly Generate and Deploy SAP Fiori Apps from ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse
entry.content:
Hi @LauraEiraElias 

Just wanted to confirm that this is not available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition?

entry.title: Adding email IDs to EWA report
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We have to add the mail ids to the PERSON_NAME report. We tried the process but it was not successful.

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Adding email IDs to EWA report
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

Please refer to the following KBAs for the steps. 

2282944 - EarlyWatch Alert: Solution Manager 7.2 how to setup/configure EWA reports or add email recipients 
2531994 - T/S Guide: EarlyWatch Alerts (EWA) reports in Solution Manager 7.2 not being sent by email 

For Setup An Email Recipient for the First Time, it needs to maintain emails to the dialog user from SU01, then add the dialog user to Global Recipient List. Once the user has been added to the Global Recipient List, you will then be able to add this user to the "Maintain Recipient Lists" button in SOLMAN_SETUP -> Application Operations -> EarlyWatch Alert Management -> Maintain Recipients. 

As we tested, it supports to add multiple emails to one dialog user. However, due to the configuration page limitation, only one email is shown in the configuration UI. 

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to Update Simulation End Macro
entry.content:
Hi Shane,

Thanks for helping me. Does this need any specific role like Master author or Admin to update the text?

entry.title: How to hide post button of "Create Supplier Invoice"
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

We would like to implement the following division of roles when using the "Create Supplier Invoice" app:

・Staff in charge: Park invoices.

・Approver: Review the parked invoice created by the staff and then post it.

Question (1)

Therefore, we would like to hide the post button from the "Create Supplier Invoice" app for specific users.
We found that we can hide the post button by removing the following business catalogs from the user's role:

  • SAP_MM_BC_INV_PROCESS_MC 
  • SAP_MM_BC_INV_PROCESS_PC 
  • SAP_MM_BC_INV_SCHEDULE_PC 
  • SAP_LO_BC_LFE_CRT_INTF_PC 
  • SAP_FIN_BC_AA_DOC_PROC_PC 
  • SAP_FIN_BC_AA_POST_REG_PC 

Is this method appropriate?

We found a related comment on SAP Community and checked the KBA, but the KBA has been deleted.

Solved: how to restrict users to post suppliers invoices - SAP Community

https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2834732

Question (2)

We believe that the restriction type "Authorization Groups for Journal Entry Document Type" in the business catalog is related to the visibility of post button.

If 'Write' is marked as X, post button is displayed.

The relevant application is specified in the "Applications" tab of the catalog.

Applications tab

FIN (Finance) SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP runtimes, my personal considerations and preferences on Cloud Foundry, Kyma, ABAP runtim
entry.content:
Hey @mauriciolauffer , nice comparison of the various runtimes, gives you a good way to assess the various trade-offs and make an informed decision.

I did feel that ABAP Cloud came off not so well, although with fair points against it. Scraping for extra points to help the ABAP column a fraction, would you consider that at least ABAP may be easier to deploy even complex applications (even though the trade off is the lack of flexibility as described)?

Thanks for blogging!

entry.title: Re: 2LIS_02_ITM did not get the deleted item of PO in ME2...
entry.content:
Hi Charlie

The field LOEKZ "Deletion Ind." in the extractor is what you are looking for. Filter on loekz = '' to get only active rows. 

Regards

Kristian

entry.title: Re: Send data from SAP Analytics Cloud to 3rd Party tools using CPI
entry.content: @akshaykhatri1992 - Nice blog
